control_description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.

evidence_files.description: Meridian Analytics HR security policy covering pre-employment screening, security awareness training, acceptable use, and termination procedures

prompt: Review the HR security policy for Meridian Analytics. Assess whether it adequately addresses SOC 2 CC1.4 requirements for attracting, developing, and retaining competent individuals with appropriate security controls. Identify any gaps or missing elements that an auditor would flag.

findings.title: No background check requirement in pre-employment screening
findings.description: Section 3 (Pre-Employment Screening) covers identity verification, reference checks, and employment history verification but does not include any requirement for criminal background checks. CC1.4 requires demonstrating a commitment to attracting competent and trustworthy individuals. Background checks are an industry-standard control for validating candidate suitability, particularly for roles with access to sensitive data or systems. The policy includes reference checks (Section 3.2) and employment history verification (Section 3.3) but omits this critical screening component. Without background checks, the organization cannot adequately assess candidate trustworthiness.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor should review Section 3 and identify that while identity verification, references, and employment history are addressed, criminal background checks are entirely absent. This is a significant omission for a policy governing access to information systems. The auditor should note that industry standards and SOC 2 expectations include background screening as a standard pre-employment control.

findings.title: No defined frequency for ongoing security awareness training
findings.description: Section 4.2 states employees must complete security awareness training 'on an ongoing basis' but does not define a specific frequency (e.g., annually, semi-annually, quarterly). CC1.4 requires demonstrating a commitment to developing competent individuals. Without a defined training frequency, there is no measurable, enforceable standard for how often employees refresh their security knowledge. An auditor cannot verify compliance with an undefined schedule. Industry standard is at minimum annual security awareness training with documented completion tracking.
findings.expected_reasoning: Section 4.2 uses the phrase 'on an ongoing basis' which is vague and unenforceable. The auditor should identify that this language does not establish a measurable training cadence. Without a specific frequency such as 'annually' or 'quarterly,' the organization cannot demonstrate consistent execution of its training program or track compliance.

findings.title: No defined schedule for training content review and updates
findings.description: Section 4.3 states training content is reviewed 'when updates are deemed necessary' rather than on a defined schedule. This subjective trigger means training materials could become outdated without any formal review mechanism. Security threats evolve continuously, and training content should be reviewed at least annually to ensure relevance. Without a defined review schedule, there is no assurance that the training program addresses current threat landscape.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor should note that Section 4.3 uses discretionary language for content updates rather than a defined schedule. Industry best practice is at minimum annual review of security awareness training content to address emerging threats. The phrase 'deemed necessary' provides no accountability or measurable standard.
